meta.name: Animals
meta.department: Finance (licensing) + Animal Control Board + Maui Humane Society (enforcement partner)
meta.description: govOS free public service: a plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 6 (Animals) — dog licensing, dangerous-dog rules, animal control, and who pays. Sourced to Municode + County; first version, expanding.
meta.eyebrow: 12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 6 (Animals) — Chapter 6.04 Animal Control: dog licensing, the rules for dangerous dogs, animal regulations, and who pays. Licensing runs through Finance; appeals on dangerous-dog determinations go to the Animal Control Board; the Maui Humane Society is the County's animal-services partner. Sections marked expanding are being filled in; nothing here is legal advice.

sections.title: 1 · License your dog
sections.note: Dog licensing is required under Ch. 6.04. Here's the path.

steps.name: Who needs a license
steps.what: No one may own, keep, or harbor a dog over four months old in the County unless it is licensed. Apply within 30 days of getting a dog over four months.
steps.pay: Expense: none to check · paid by: you (time)

steps.name: What to bring
steps.what: Picture ID; the dog's name, breed, color, age, and sex; a veterinarian's certificate if claiming the spay/neuter rate; and the fee.
steps.pay: Expense: vet visit (if needed) · paid by: owner

steps.name: Pay the fee
steps.what: $11 with proof the dog is spayed/neutered; $76 if unaltered. Late applicants owe a delinquent fee set in the annual budget ordinance.
steps.pay: Expense: license fee · paid by: owner

position.label: ⚖️ Position — James Langford (analysis, not law; a stance beside the rule)
position.text: The big gap between the $11 altered and $76 unaltered fee is good policy — it prices in the public cost of unplanned litters and overcrowded shelters. Keep licensing cheap and easy for responsible owners; the fee should encourage spay/neuter, not punish pet ownership.

source.html: Maui County Code §6.04.020 (Licensing); fees $11 (altered) / $76 (unaltered); licensing authority is the Department of Finance. Partner: Maui Humane Society.

sections.title: 2 · Dog license fees
sections.note: Set in Ch. 6.04 / the annual budget ordinance. Confirm current amounts before paying.

source.html: Maui County Code Ch. 6.04 (Animal Control).

sections.title: 3 · Dangerous dogs
sections.note: Special rules apply to a dog deemed dangerous. Not legal advice.
sections.html: A dangerous dog is any dog deemed dangerous by an enforcement or animal-control officer (§6.04.046). Owners of a dangerous dog face special containment, signage, and insurance requirements; failure to comply carries a maximum penalty of $1,000 and/or 30 days in jail. A determination can be appealed to the Animal Control Board (§§6.04.046, 6.04.050).

source.html: Maui County Code §6.04.046 / §6.04.050; Animal Control Board.

sections.title: 4 · Who pays — the charter lens
sections.note: Labeled analysis — animal law read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter, the County licenses animals through Finance, regulates dangerous dogs, and partners with the Humane Society for shelter and enforcement; fees fund the program. The Charter frames animal control as a public-safety and welfare function.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, animals are part of the household and the living ʻāina; stewardship is a kuleana. The lens favors low-cost licensing, strong spay/neuter incentives, and humane treatment over punitive fees, and reads the dangerous-dog rules as protecting both people and animals from harm.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: Make responsible ownership the easy, cheap path; reserve real penalties for genuine danger. The $11/$76 split already does this — keep it. Same title, read two ways.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this page, the licensing navigator, and the fee table are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a license-renewal reminder and an AI animal-code assistant will be verification-gated via the free govOS beta portal (identity verified with Stripe Identity — free, no charge); only verified-status is stored, never the raw ID. No secret keys (environment references only); any license payment happens on the County's own system.
